notifier._comment_type: Which push backend to use: 'bark' (iPhone/Apple Watch), 'pushover', or 'ntfy'. Only the fields for the chosen type matter; the others can stay as placeholders.
notifier._comment_pushover: type=pushover: create an app at pushover.net/apps for pushover_token; pushover_user is the user key on your dashboard. pushover_priority is optional (-2..2, omit/0 = normal). pushover_server only changes for self-hosted setups.
notifier._comment_ntfy: type=ntfy: ntfy_topic is your subscribed topic. ntfy_server defaults to public https://ntfy.sh (set your self-hosted URL otherwise). ntfy_priority 1-5 (5=max, default 4). ntfy_token only needed for protected/private topics (Bearer auth).

notification_policy._comment: actionable = only push when user interaction is needed; verbose = push every event
notification_policy._comment_pending_bg: task_done_pending_bg fires when the main turn stops while dispatched background tasks are still running; log-only by default to avoid false 'task complete' pushes. Set true to be notified anyway.

away_mode._comment: When you step away, run `agentwatch away on` to suppress normal pushes (task done, etc.) and `agentwatch away off` to resume. Critical events — repeated failures and interaction prompts — always push through even while away. 'enabled' is the master switch for this whole layer (set false to make `away on` a no-op); the actual on/off state is toggled at runtime via the CLI, not stored here. 'extra_critical_events' lists additional event_types that should bypass away suppression.
away_mode._comment_schedule: Optional do-not-disturb windows that auto-suppress normal pushes during the listed hours (local time), no manual toggle needed. Composes with manual away as a plain OR — a manual `away off` does NOT lift an active scheduled window. Set schedule.enabled=false (default) for pure-manual behavior. Each window is "HH:MM-HH:MM"; a start later than end (e.g. 23:00-08:00) wraps past midnight.
